contributor: university of illinois at urbana-champaign library, urbana, il
description: Chinese Red Army in Province of Honan Surrounds Kwangchow Tension Increases at Hankow as Mass Up rising Threatens Japanese and Kuomintang Japanese Suffer SetBacks at Shanghai and In Several Manchurian Towns A Washington dispatch reports that a Chinese Red Army force is beseiging the important town of Hupeh province where several Chinese Red Armies are grimly tightening their net around the giant Yangtze River port of Hankow site of one of the two great arsenals in China The United States imperialists have dispatched orders to their Kuo mintang tools to send an army a gainst ttye Chinese Red Armies Sev eral missionary agents of imperial ism are reported cooped up in Kw angchow by the besieging Red Army Rear of a mass uprising at Han kow and Ichang is expressed in im perialist press dispatches from Shanghai The tension between the Chinese masses and the Japanese forces in these cities has grown sharply during the past few days as a result of the heroic resistance at Shanghai of Chinese workers and soldiers fighting in defiance of the orders of the Nanking Kuomintang government The dispatch says that the Japanese concession at Han kow has become a fortified war camp Japanese
